title: Fuengirola Catamaran Charter Sunset Guide: Costs, Routes & Tips for 2026
html:
Planning a catamaran charter sunset in Fuengirola? Boats depart from Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, with shared sunset tours from €49 per person and private charters from €600. BoatHire24 connects you with local operators.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran for sunset in Fuengirola?

Shared sunset tours start at €49 per person, while a private 4-hour charter costs around €600, €1,200. For a full-day catamaran charter, expect to pay from €1,500. You can compare skippered charters in Fuengirola on skippered charters in Fuengirola to find the right vessel.

Which boat to rent

  1. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), Best for family groups or couples; up to 12 guests; from €600 for half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 55), Sleek and fast; up to 10 guests; from €800 for half-day.
  3. Sailboat (e.g. Sun Odyssey 349), Ideal for sailing purists; up to 8 guests; from €400 for half-day.
  4. Skippered catamaran (e.g. Sanyati Leopard 51ft), Crewed with skipper; up to 12 guests; from €900 for half-day. For full details, see the Sanyati charter page.

Where to set off in Fuengirola

Most sunset charters leave from Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, a modern marina with ample parking and restaurants. Other departure points include Puerto de la Duquesa (15 nm west) and Puerto Banús in Marbella (10 nm east). Popular cruising areas include the coast towards Calahonda and the bay of Mijas. The nearby island of Isla de las Palomas is often passed on the way to Cabopino.

Cost breakdown for boat rental in Fuengirola

  • Shared sunset tour (2 hours): €49, €79 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (without skipper): from €250
  • Catamaran full-day private (up to 12 guests): from €1,500
  • Luxury crewed yacht (e.g. Mangusta 80): from €3,000 per day

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Fuengirola?

In Spain, a licence is required for bareboat charters on boats over 5 metres or with more than 15 hp. However, most sunset catamaran charters include a licensed skipper, so no licence is needed. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking.

If you charter a skippered boat, the captain will choose the best anchorage for sunset, often near Calahonda or the cliffs of Mijas. Trust their local knowledge for the best golden-hour views.

Season and weather for sunset charters

The peak season for sunset boat trips runs from May to September, with July and August offering the warmest evenings. The prevailing wind in the Costa del Sol is the Poniente, a gentle westerly that usually dies down by dusk, creating calm sea conditions.

At a glance

  • Price band: €49, €3,000 per trip
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Max guests: 12 on standard catamarans
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Fuengirola

Can I charter a catamaran for just two people in Fuengirola?

Yes, many operators offer private charters with a minimum booking of two guests, though shared tours are also available.

How far in advance should I book a sunset charter?

For peak season (June to August), book at least two weeks ahead to secure your preferred date and vessel.

What is included in the price of a catamaran charter?

Most skippered charters include fuel, a captain, and sometimes snacks or drinks; confirm with the operator.

Is it possible to swim during a sunset trip?

Yes, if the itinerary includes a stop in a sheltered bay such as Cabopino or La Cala de Mijas.

The sunset hours between 20:00 and 21:30 in summer offer the best light, and many charters include a dip in the Mediterranean before the golden hour. Book through the BoatHire24 marketplace to compare verified boats. Boat owners can also list their vessel and reach thousands of travellers planning their Fuengirola boat trip in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fuengirola and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
